papers.title: CoME-VL: Scaling Complementary Multi-Encoder Vision-Language Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract A vision-language model fusion framework combines contrastive and self-supervised visual encoders using entropy-guided aggregation and RoPE-enhanced attention to improve visual understanding and grounding tasks.  AI-generated summary Recent vision-language models (VLMs) typically rely on a single vision encoder trained with contrastive image-text objectives, such as CLIP-style pretraining. While contrastive encoders are effective for cross-modal alignment and retrieval, self-supervised visual encoders often capture richer dense semantics and exhibit stronger robustness on recognition and understanding tasks. In this work, we investigate how to scale the fusion of these complementary visual representations for vision-language modeling. We propose CoME-VL: Complementary Multi-Encoder Vision-Language, a modular fusion framework that integrates a contrastively trained vision encoder with a self-supervised DINO encoder. Our approach performs representation-level fusion by (i) entropy-guided multi-layer aggregation with orthogonality-constrained projections to reduce redundancy, and (ii) RoPE-enhanced cross-attention to align heterogeneous token grids and produce compact fused visual tokens. The fused tokens can be injected into a decoder-only LLM with minimal changes to standard VLM pipelines. Extensive experiments across diverse vision-language benchmarks demonstrate that CoME-VL consistently outperforms single-encoder baselines. In particular, we observe an average improvement of 4.9% on visual understanding tasks and 5.4% on grounding tasks. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on RefCOCO for detection while improving over the baseline by a large margin. Finally, we conduct ablation studies on layer merging, non-redundant feature mixing, and fusion capacity to evaluate how complementary contrastive and self-supervised signals affect VLM performance.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence Abu Dhabi, UAE

ru.title: Слияние дополнительных визуальных представлений для лучшего понимания изображений
ru.desc: Работа предлагает CoME-VL — фреймворк для объединения двух типов визуальных энкодеров: контrastive-обученного (как CLIP) и self-supervised (DINO), которые захватывают дополнительную визуальную информацию. Авторы используют энтропийную агрегацию с ортогональными проекциями для снижения избыточности признаков и RoPE-enhanced кросс-внимание для выравнивания разнородных сеток токенов из разных энкодеров. Полученные фузионированные визуальные токены интегрируются в decoder-only LLM с минимальными изменениями стандартного pipeline. Экспериментальные результаты показывают улучшение на 4.9% в задачах визуального понимания и 5.4% в задачах grounding по сравнению с однозначными baseline'ами.

en.title: Fusing Visual Encoders for Superior Vision-Language Understanding
en.desc: This paper presents CoME-VL, a novel framework that fuses contrastive and self-supervised visual encoders to enhance vision-language tasks. By employing entropy-guided aggregation and RoPE-enhanced attention, the model effectively reduces redundancy and aligns diverse visual representations. The integration of these complementary encoders leads to improved performance in visual understanding and grounding tasks, surpassing traditional single-encoder models. Experimental results show significant gains, with an average improvement of 4.9% in understanding tasks and 5.4% in grounding tasks, establishing state-of-the-art results in specific benchmarks.

zh.title: 视觉与语言的完美融合
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种视觉-语言模型融合框架，旨在通过熵引导聚合和增强注意力机制来提升视觉理解和定位任务的效果。我们提出的CoME-VL框架结合了对比训练的视觉编码器和自监督的DINO编码器，实现了多层次的表示融合。通过减少冗余和对异构标记网格的对齐，生成紧凑的融合视觉标记。实验结果表明，CoME-VL在多个视觉-语言基准测试中表现优于单一编码器基线，特别是在视觉理解和定位任务上有显著提升。

papers.title: InCoder-32B-Thinking: Industrial Code World Model for Thinking
papers.abstract: Abstract Industrial software development lacks expert reasoning traces for hardware constraints, so a model was trained on error-driven reasoning chains and domain-specific execution traces to generate high-quality code reasoning and performance.  AI-generated summary Industrial software development across chip design, GPU optimization, and embedded systems lacks expert reasoning traces showing how engineers reason about hardware constraints and timing semantics. In this work, we propose InCoder-32B-Thinking, trained on the data from the Error-driven Chain-of-Thought (ECoT) synthesis framework with an industrial code world model (ICWM) to generate reasoning traces. Specifically, ECoT generates reasoning chains by synthesizing the thinking content from multi-turn dialogue with environmental error feedback, explicitly modeling the error-correction process. ICWM is trained on domain-specific execution traces from Verilog simulation, GPU profiling, etc., learns the causal dynamics of how code affects hardware behavior, and enables self-verification by predicting execution outcomes before actual compilation. All synthesized reasoning traces are validated through domain toolchains, creating training data matching the natural reasoning depth distribution of industrial tasks. Evaluation on 14 general (81.3% on LiveCodeBench v5) and 9 industrial benchmarks (84.0% in CAD-Coder and 38.0% on KernelBench) shows InCoder-32B-Thinking achieves top-tier open-source results across all domains.GPU Optimization
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China

ru.title: Обучение моделей кода экспертному рассуждению об аппаратных ограничениях через цепочки, управляемые ошибками
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали InCoder-32B-Thinking, модель на основе трансформера, обученную на цепочках рассуждений, ошибки которых были исправлены автоматически (ECoT). Модель дополнена промышленной моделью мира кода (ICWM), которая изучает причинно-следственные связи между кодом и поведением оборудования, используя следы выполнения из Verilog и GPU профилирования. Система может самопроверяться, предсказывая результаты выполнения до компиляции, что позволяет генерировать высококачественные цепочки рассуждений для задач проектирования чипов и оптимизации GPU. На промышленных бенчмарках модель достигла лучших результатов среди открытых моделей, показав 84% на CAD-Coder и 81.3% на LiveCodeBench.

en.title: Empowering Industrial Software with Expert Reasoning Traces
en.desc: This paper presents InCoder-32B-Thinking, a model designed to enhance industrial software development by generating expert reasoning traces for hardware constraints. It utilizes the Error-driven Chain-of-Thought (ECoT) synthesis framework, which creates reasoning chains based on multi-turn dialogues and environmental error feedback. Additionally, the model incorporates an industrial code world model (ICWM) trained on domain-specific execution traces, allowing it to understand the causal relationships between code and hardware behavior. The evaluation demonstrates that InCoder-32B-Thinking achieves high performance on various benchmarks, indicating its effectiveness in generating high-quality code reasoning and improving software development processes.

zh.title: 智能推理，提升工业软件开发
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为InCoder-32B-Thinking的模型，旨在解决工业软件开发中缺乏专家推理痕迹的问题。该模型通过错误驱动的推理链和领域特定的执行轨迹进行训练，以生成高质量的代码推理和性能。具体而言，模型利用多轮对话中的思维内容和环境错误反馈，明确建模错误修正过程，从而生成推理链。通过在Verilog仿真和GPU分析等领域特定执行轨迹上进行训练，模型能够预测执行结果，实现自我验证。
